title: Style Form State without Premature Blame
summary: Build and verify css form state pseudo classes in survey validation and selection-state timing review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css form state pseudo classes from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css form state pseudo classes without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

content.text: Form pseudo-classes include :required, :optional, :valid, :invalid, :user-valid, :user-invalid, :checked, :indeterminate, :enabled, :disabled, :read-only, and :placeholder-shown. Native validity reflects constraints, not whether a user has interacted. User-state selectors or a submitted-state class can delay strong error styling. Color must be paired with text, icons, or boundaries. Apply it to survey validation and selection-state timing review.
